publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Thursday November 23, 2023 at 04:01

did.unittitle: Henry Walke artwork

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: The collection consists of watercolors, lithographs, oil paintings, and drawings by American naval officer Henry Walke, depicting scenes from the American Civil War and Mexican-American War, as well as landscapes and maritime scenes from expeditions with the United States Navy to Europe, South America, the Middle East, and Africa.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from H.W. Jesse Jenckes on the Fedrick W. and Carrie S. Beinecke Fund for Western Americana, 2018.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into two series: I. Artwork, 1827-circa 1877, undated. II. Papers, 1861-1917, undated.

bioghist.head: Henry Walke (1808-1896)
bioghist.p: Rear Admiral Henry Walke was an American naval officer and artist born in Princess Anne County, Virginia, to Anthony Walke, a Yale College graduate and former American diplomat, and Susan Carmichael Walke. During the Mexican-American War, he was executive officer of the bomb-brig Vesuvius , which supported U.S. Army blockades of the Gulf ports of Veracruz, Alvarado, Tuxpan, and Villahermosa. In 1847 and 1848, Sarony & Major of New York City published his Naval Portfolio: Naval Scenes in the Mexican War by H. Walke, lieut., U.S. Navy . In the early part of the Civil War, Walke commanded the USS Tyler and USS Carondelet in the Western Theater, supporting Union forces during the Battle of Belmont and operations against Fort Henry, Fort Donelson, and Island Number 10. Walke also commanded the USS Lafayette , assigned to the Union Army's Mississippi River Squadron during the Battle of Vicksburg and the Red River Campaign. After serving more than 40 years in the Navy, Walke retired from active duty in 1873 and moved to Brooklyn, New York, where he worked as a writer and artist until his death. His watercolor and oil paintings were primarily based on his sketches of landscapes and maritime scenes, including Civil War engagements at Belmont, Fort Henry, and Grand Gulf. He authored and illustrated Naval Scenes and Reminiscences of the Civil War (1877).

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Henry Walke Artwork. Yale Collection of Western Americana, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.

Information included in the Description of Papers note and Collection Contents section is drawn from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents. Most folder titles appearing in the contents list below were transcribed from the recto or verso of the artwork. Otherwise, folder titles were supplied by staff during processing.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection and/or revisions in arrangement and description.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The collection consists of watercolors, lithographs, oil paintings, and drawings by American naval officer Henry Walke, depicting scenes from the American Civil War and Mexican-American War, as well as expeditions with the United States Navy to Europe, South America, the Middle East, and Africa. Included are paintings of Union ships commanded by Walke, such as the USS Carondelet , and Civil War battles of Belmont, Fort Donelson, Fort Henry, Island Number Ten, and Grand Gulf. The collection also contains paintings and drawings of maritime scenes, landscapes, ancient ruins, monuments, buildings, and religious sites in Angola, Australia, Brazil, Equatorial Guinea, Ireland, Israel, Italy, South Africa, and Syria. Additionally included are a few photographs, broadsides, and printed material by or related to Walke.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Henry Walke Artwork is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

did.unittitle: Artwork

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains paintings, lithographs, and drawings depicting scenes from the Western Theater of the Civil War, United States naval expeditions under the command of M. C. Perry during the Mexican-American War, and various locations visited by Henry Walke during his naval career, in addition to landscapes and pastoral scenes from America.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into three subseries: Civil War, Mexican-American War, and Other.

did.unittitle: Civil War

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries contains Walke's paintings, lithographs, and drawings of early Civil War battles, engagements of the Mississippi River Squadron, and scenes of vessels he commanded, such as the Carondelet and Tyler.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized under three subheadings: Paintings, Lithographs, and Drawings. Within each subheading, materials maintain order found at time of acquisition.

did.unittitle: Paintings
Naval Battle of Belmont with people and horses at home in foreground
Carondelet - Confederate Ram Fleet - Flag Steamer Benton - Cincinnati - Mound City - St. Louis - Pittsburg - Cairo
Four gun and eight guns Battle of Ft. Donelson
Battle of Ft. Henry, Kentucky
U.S. Gunboat Carondelet capturing the Confederate Forts below Island #10, April 6, 1862
Battle of Ft. Donelson, Feb 13, 1862 - U.S. Gunboat Carondelet
June 5, 1862 Randolph - Mississippi River - Confederate Gunboats searching for a canoe bearing dispatches to Admiral Farragut
U.S. Flotilla Bombarding the Confederate batteries above Island No 10 March 17, 1862
"This view of running the gauntlet at Vicksburg is very good and true to era" - John S. Tennyson [written on verso]
Battle of Belmont
Battle of Fort Henry (No. 2)
Carondelet on a reconnoiter of the Yazoo July 15th 1862
On board the U S Gunboat Taylor [sic] in the battle of Belmont, Mississippi River

Queen of the West - Taylor - the Carondelet and R. Ram Arkansas parting company on the Yazoo River, July 15th 1862
The Rebel Ram Arkansas closely engaged with the U S Gunboat Carondelet in the Yazoo River July 15th 1862
Capturing the rebel batteries below Island No 10 April 7th 1862
The Confederate Gunboat Alabama, sunk by the U S Gunboat Kearsarge in the British Channel off Cherbourg, France June 19, 1864
Porter's Mortar Fleet - Farragut's fleet passing the fort New Orleans
Farragut passing Fort Hudson - Hartford - Mississippi - Richmond
Battle of Mobile
Battle of New Orleans
The U States Flotilla under the Command of R Admiral DD Porter passing Vicksburg Batteries April 16th 1863
The Battle of Grand Gulf, 2nd position about 11 o'clock am
The Battle of Grand Gulf 1st Position April 29th 1863 at 10 o'clock am
Interior battle scene on board of the U States Ram Gunboat Carondelet Com H Walke - Battle of Fort Donelson Febry 11th 1862
United States Gunboat Carondelet passing the rebel batteries at Island Number Ten April 4th 1862
The Retreating Confederate fleet at the Battle of Memphis June 6th 1862
Rebels vessels on fire - City of Memphis in the distance June 6th 1862
Battle of Memphis June 6th 1862
Rebel tug on fire - U S Flotilla at anchor - City of Memphis - June 5th 1862
Fort Pillow June 5, 1862 - Looking East
Battle of Fort Pillow - Mississippi River May the 10th 1862 - Looking North
Battle of Belmont 3d position of the U S Gunboats Nov 7, 1862
Bombardment of the rebels above Island No 10 March 17, 1862
Untitled [Carondelet near Island No. 10]
The Confederate Steamer Florida running the blockade into Mobile
Two sailing ships at dark in rough water
June 5, 1862 - Tug in chase of the "Sovereign" Benton - St Louis - Carondelet [following the tug] - Mississippi Flotilla, Capt C H Davis Commanding
U S Flotilla Flag Officer Foote Commanding on chase of rebel gunboat Grampus, on the way to Island No 10 March 15, 1862

U S Gunboat Carondelet Capturing the Forts below Island No 10 April 6th 1862

Evacuation of Pensacola and Reinforcements of Fort Pickens
Battle of Belmont Nov 7th 1861 Viewed from the North West River at low water mark
Running the gauntlet of Island No 10.; writing and drawing on verso
Mexican-American War

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries contains a complete set of Walke's hand-colored lithographs of naval scenes from the Mexican-American War under the command of M. C. Perry, as well as four paintings depicting the conflict.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized under two subheadings: Lithographs and Paintings. Within each subheading, materials maintain order found at time of acquisition.

did.unittitle: Lithographs
Lithograph No 1 – U.S Steam Frigate Mississippi, Com M. C. Perry
Lithograph No 2 – The U. S. Naval Expedition Com M. C. Perry
Lithograph No 3 – U. States Steamers, Spitfire, Vixen and Scourge, with 40 Barges in Tow Crossing the Bar at the Mouth of Tabasco River, Mexico
Lithograph No 4 – The Attack of the Mexicans from the Chapperal, on the First Division of the Naval Expedition to Tabasco (Mexico)
Lithograph No 5 – The Naval Expedition under Com Perry, Ascending the Tabasco River at the Devils Bend, June 15th 1847
Lithograph No 6 – The Landing of the Naval Expedition, against Tabasco (Mexico) Com M. C. Perry in Command
Lithograph No 7 – The Capture of the City of Tabasco by the U.S. Naval Expedition, afloat and on shore, under Com M. C. Perry
Lithograph No 8 – The U.S. Naval Battery During the Bombardment of Vera Cruz on the 24 and 25 of March 1847
Paintings
U S Squadron Commodore M C Perry Starting for Tuspan [sic], Mexico April 1847
Harbor of Laguna South Coast of Mexico 1847
Commodore M. C. Perry's Expedition to Tuspan [sic], Mexico 1847
N S Brig Sumers capsized off Vera Cruz Dec 1847 during the U States War with Mexico
Other

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries contains paintings, lithographs, and drawings by Walke of naval engagements or expeditions of American, British, and French ships; harbors and ports, coastlines, pastoral scenes, and mountains; and churches, castles, forts, and other cultural landmarks. Included are paintings of Mount Vesuvius and Pompeii, the harbor of Rio de Janeiro (known as Guanabara Bay), the Andes Mountains, Rachel's Tomb in Bethlehem, and Gethsemane in Jerusalem. In addition, the subseries contains one oil painting of Walke by an unknown artist.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized under three subheadings: Paintings, Lithographs, and Drawings. Within each subheading, materials maintain order found at time of acquisition.

Untitled drawing of ships in harbor with intricate town in background
Crater of Fayal [sic] Western Islands (Portuguese Azores)
Papers

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series consists of broadsides related to Henry Walke's naval career, photographs, published works including his Naval Scenes on the Western Waters and a Civil War-era guide to the Mississippi River, and early 20th century articles about the launch of the USS Walke.

did.unittitle: Broadsides
List of Walke battles in the Mexican & Civil Wars
List of Walke promotions and dates
List of naval vessels and dates commanded by Walke
Photographs
Gunboat Carondelet presented to Captain Henry Walke USN with compliments of the Constructor, Samuel B Eady
Walke home in Chillicothe, Ohio
Walke in retirement
Printed Material
Harper's Weekly: Journal of Civilization (1862), magazine cover of "Aerial view of Island No 10 - The bombardment of Island #10" by Henry Walke [copy]
Naval Scenes on the Western Waters: The Gunboats Taylor, Carondelet and Lafayette by Henry Walke

title.part: The United Service: A Monthly Review of Military and Naval Affairs
Battles and Leaders of the Civil War - People's Pictorial Edition, Part III

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: With Walke's account of the operations of the Mississippi River Squadron during the Civil War, including copies of his drawings of different ships and scenes.

did.unittitle: Clippings

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Subjects include launch of USS Walke and Mildred Walke Walter, Walke's granddaughter.
